title: 100x M2 medium
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"a3b","text":"This protocol describes how to prepare a 100x M2","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"850pv","text":"1x M2 media could be created from the stock and supplemental stocks and trace metals could be added afterwards.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ck74l","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5lfp8","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5jcgj","text":"Recipes for standard and alternative M2 for culturing freshwater cyanobacteria, such as Synechocystis sp. PCC 6803, in a co-culture togther with heterotrophic bacteria.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":88,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5gqqr","text":"Media is usually not suitable for marine cyanobacteria.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"iidf","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9sjk8","text":"Slighly modified from: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e32q0","text":"Weiss, Taylor L., Eric J. Young, and Daniel C. Ducat. \"A synthetic,  light-driven consortium of cyanobacteria and heterotrophic bacteria  enables stable polyhydroxybutyrate production.\" Metabolic engineering 44 (2017): 236-245.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":186,"length":21}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Add CaCl 2 2H 2 O (3.6 g · L -1 ) If you do not have hydrated CaCl 2, use 2.718 g CaCl 2 (powder) for 1L of the stock. 0.25 M Na 2 -EDTA stock: Dissolve 2.32 g Na 2 -EDTA (powder) and fill the bottle up with MilliQ (nuclease-free) water up to 25 mL. Afterwards, the pH was adjusted with diluted NaOH to pH = 8.0. K2HPO4 (82,3g·L -1 )
Add MgSO4 · 7 H2O (7.49 g · L-1) If you do not have hydrated MgSO4, use 3.659 g MgSO4 (powder) for 1L of the stock.
Fill up with MiliQ water to one liter
Mix the solution with a stir bar
creator: Sarah Seyffert
